subject: Economics
overall_learning_objectives: Critically analyze the 3-A framework (Availability, Accessibility, Affordability) in the context of global food summits
Evaluate the 'Entitlement' approach to food security and the causes of modern-day famines
Analyze the fiscal and logistical challenges of the Food Corporation of India (FCI) and Buffer Stock norms
Deconstruct the structural issues of the Public Distribution System (PDS) and the TPDS transition
Assess the impact of the National Food Security Act (2013) on legal rights and nutritional outcomes
Synthesize the role of decentralized actors (Cooperatives/NGOs) in ensuring sustainable food sovereignty

lesson_plan.title: Theoretical Framework: The Entitlement Approach
topics_covered: 1996 World Food Summit definition: Beyond calories to 'Active and Healthy Life'
Amartya Sen’s theory of 'Exchange Entitlements'
Case Study Audit: 1943 Bengal Famine as a failure of access, not availability
teaching_strategy: Comparative analysis of the starvation process during droughts
student_activities: Analyze a cause-effect flow of the Bengal Famine
Draft a thesis on why 'Self-sufficiency' doesn't guarantee 'Food Security'
assessment: Peer review of the cause-effect flow

lesson_plan.title: Socio-Spatial Vulnerability & Hunger
topics_covered: The Geography of Hunger: Analyzing food-insecure pockets in India
Intersectionality of Caste, Gender, and Food Insecurity
Chronic vs. Seasonal Hunger: A structural analysis of labor markets
teaching_strategy: Advanced mapping of regional food disparities
student_activities: Analyze the 'Vulnerability Matrix' of rural artisans and casual laborers
Graph the seasonal hunger cycles in mono-crop agricultural regions
assessment: Data interpretation test on vulnerability ratios

lesson_plan.title: The Political Economy of the Green Revolution
topics_covered: Shift from 'PL-480' imports to the 'Wheat Revolution'
Regional production concentration: Punjab/Haryana vs. The Rest
Ecological externalities of intensive food production
teaching_strategy: Data-driven lecture on crop production trends since 1960
Discussion on the 'Sustainability' of current grain production
student_activities: Interpret complex bar graphs of food grain stocks vs. production
Critique the focus on wheat/rice over coarse grains (millets)
assessment: Comparative report on regional agricultural disparities

lesson_plan.title: Logistics of Security: FCI & Buffer Stock
topics_covered: The economics of Minimum Support Price (MSP) and Open-Ended Procurement
Buffer Stock norms vs. Actual Stocks: The 'Carrying Cost' challenge
FCI’s logistical chain: Procurement, Storage, and Transportation
teaching_strategy: Flowcharting the 'Grain Mountain': Why stock piles up while hunger persists
Lecture on the fiscal deficit and the food subsidy bill
student_activities: Calculate the 'Food Subsidy' as the gap between MSP+Storage and Issue Price
Map the logistics of grain movement from surplus to deficit states
assessment: Numerical problem set on food subsidies

lesson_plan.title: PDS Audit: Targeting and Leakages
topics_covered: The TPDS transition (1997): Identifying 'Inclusion' and 'Exclusion' errors
Institutional failures: Grain diversion, corruption, and the role of Aadhaar
The Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY): Targeting the ultra-poor
teaching_strategy: Critical audit of Fair Price Shop (FPS) operations
student_activities: Analyze a report on PDS leakages in a specific state
Draft a policy brief to minimize 'Exclusion Errors' in BPL card distribution

lesson_plan.title: Legal Entitlements: NFSA 2013
topics_covered: National Food Security Act (2013): Shift from Welfare to Rights
Nutritional standards for ICDS, Mid-day Meals, and PDS
Maternity benefits and the role of Aanganwadis
teaching_strategy: Lecture on the legal framework of the 'Right to Food'
Brainstorming the impact of legal mandates on state accountability
student_activities: Evaluate the impact of the Mid-day meal as a 'Social Safety Net'
Design an awareness campaign for the legal rights under NFSA

lesson_plan.title: Sustainable Models & Synthesis
topics_covered: Decentralization: Grain Banks (NGOs) and Cooperatives (Amul/Mother Dairy)
The Role of the 'Academy of Development Science' (ADS) in Maharashtra
Chapter Synthesis: The Future of Zero Hunger
teaching_strategy: Case study discussion on decentralized grain management
Final mind-mapping session: 'Achieving SDG 2: Zero Hunger'
student_activities: Participate in a whole-chapter 'Grand Revision' seminar
Complete a summary synthesis chart in notebooks
assessment: Comprehensive unit test (30 marks) featuring case-study based questions

teaching_aids: Complex data sets on Gini coefficient and grain storage norms
Flowcharts for PDS and FCI logistics
India map showing food grain production vs. hunger indices
Case study handouts on Amul and NGO-led grain banks
evaluation_methods: Advanced Data Interpretation and Numerical Solving
teacher_tip: Focus on the 'Fiscal vs. Social' dilemma—explain how the government must balance high procurement prices for farmers' livelihood with low issue prices for consumers' survival.
